title: Agenomic Genome (v0.2)
description: Declarative description of an agent's design: identity, runtime, capabilities, knowledge references, policies, and memory references. v0.2 adds optional orchestration-facing fields (triggers, autonomy, guardrails, escalation rules, collaboration) so a genome can declare how it participates in workflows and multi-agent systems (RFC 0009). Every valid v0.1 genome remains valid under v0.2 after updating spec_version.

spec_version.description: Pins the Agenomic specification version this genome targets.

id.description: Stable agent identifier of the form agent://<org>/<name>.

domain.description: Free-form domain tag (e.g. claims, support, trading-risk).

task.description: Optional inference task hint for providers that distinguish tasks (e.g. Hugging Face): text-generation, embeddings, classification, etc. Absence means the provider's default task for the model.

revision.description: Optional model revision (branch, tag, or commit) requested in the genome. For Hugging Face this maps to a repo revision such as `main` or a commit SHA. The lockfile pins the resolved commit.

endpoint_url.description: Optional dedicated/self-hosted inference endpoint URL (e.g. a Hugging Face Inference Endpoint). MUST NOT contain inline credentials; authentication is supplied at runtime via environment variables, never stored in the artifact.

organization.description: Optional provider organization/namespace the model is billed or scoped to (e.g. a Hugging Face org).

parameters.description: Optional provider-specific generation parameters (e.g. Hugging Face `max_new_tokens`, `top_p`, `repetition_penalty`). Free-form object; the lockfile records a `parameter_hash` over the resolved values.

design_memory.description: Reference to the bundled design memory artifact (e.g. file path, URI, or structured ref).

runtime_memory_schema_version.description: Version string of the runtime memory schema this agent expects (e.g. 1.4.0). Runtime memory itself is NEVER bundled; only its schema version is referenced.

triggers.description: Optional declaration of how this agent is invoked. Absence means the invocation mode is unspecified.

autonomy.description: Optional explicit autonomy envelope: actions the agent may take on its own, and actions that always require escalation or are forbidden outright.

guardrails.description: Optional named output guardrails the agent's responses must respect (e.g. formal_address, no_payment_commitment). Names are free-form tags resolved by policies and behavior contracts.

escalation_rules.description: Optional declarative escalation table: when `condition` holds, the run is routed to `route` (a human role, team, or queue).

collaboration.description: Optional declaration of how this agent participates in multi-agent systems (RFC 0009).

member_of.description: Systems this agent is designed to run inside.

hand_off_to.description: Agents this agent may hand work off to.

accepts_handoffs_from.description: Agents allowed to hand work off to this agent.

forbidden_decisions.description: Optional list of decisions this agent must never take, used by behavior contracts and policies.

event.description: `event` triggers only: the event name that starts a run.

signal.description: `signal` triggers only: the signal name that starts or resumes a run.

condition.description: Free-form condition tag or expression (e.g. bodily_injury, suspected_fraud).

route.description: Human role, team, or queue that receives the escalation.
